meta.prompt: Minor refresh — added getting_started section with three lifecycle moments (Scaffold/Build/Deploy); machine name no longer prompted at scaffold time

title: What is AppySentinel?
subtitle: A boilerplate for always-on local data coordinators
definition: AppySentinel is a single-host, observer-only, always-on local data coordinator boilerplate. A Sentinel runs on one host — collecting data from local sources and from remote machines via collectors, normalising it into a common envelope, making it accessible via the Access zone (API / CLI / MCP), and delivering it outward to downstream systems.

core_rules.detail: No UI. Visualisation is a separate Viewer application that consumes the Sentinel through its access surface. Conflating data coordination with visualisation is the failure mode AppySentinel exists to prevent.
Sentinels read, never mutate the systems they observe. They are first in the boot sequence and last to shut down.
No dashboard or central system is required for a Sentinel to function. It runs and collects whether or not anything is listening.

A Sentinel runs on one host. The intended pattern is one Sentinel per machine — each knowing its local environment. A Sentinel can also reach out to remote sources (databases, APIs, other servers via SSH), but that is a capability, not the primary design. The AppyRadar SSH-orchestration (one Sentinel polling 5 machines) is a pragmatic shortcut around multi-machine install complexity, not the target architecture.

three_zones.name: Collect
three_zones.description: Read data from the outside world — SSH poll, file watch, HTTP poll, SQL diff, subprocess wrap, webhook receiver.

three_zones.name: Access
three_zones.description: Bidirectional interface layer. Query = read (pure functions over snapshots). Command = write (sentinel self-management only, opt-in). Bindings are thin adapters: api-binding, cli-binding, mcp-binding.

three_zones.name: Deliver
three_zones.description: Push data outward to downstream systems — HTTP push, file relay, Supabase, OTLP, Socket.io.

moments.label: Scaffold
moments.description: CLI copies the template, substitutes project name, runs bun install and git init. You get a working skeleton with service registration scripts already included.

moments.label: Build
moments.description: Wire recipes into main.ts — collectors, storage, expose surfaces. Run with bun, develop with watch mode. Pre-push hook blocks broken code automatically.

moments.label: Deploy
moments.description: Run install-service.sh. The Sentinel registers as a launchd (macOS) or systemd (Linux) service — starts at boot, restarts on crash, zero manual supervision.

vs_appystack.appystack: Builds web apps — React + Vite + Express + TypeScript + Socket.io (RVETS). Has a UI by definition.
vs_appystack.appysentinel: Builds always-on local data coordinators — headless, long-running, lightweight. Viewer apps that visualise Sentinel data are built separately, typically on AppyStack.

v1_pilots.name: AppyRadar Sentinel
v1_pilots.description: Fleet dashboard data half — SSH-polls 5 machines, zero remote footprint. Proves the orchestrator-ssh + snapshot-store pattern.

v1_pilots.name: SS Data Query Sentinel
v1_pilots.description: SupportSignal DB mirror — polls Supabase by updated_at watermark, exposes local mirror via MCP for AI agents. Proves the sql-diff-collector + mcp-binding pattern.
